treatment_outlook: a limited 5‑year survival (~8% for advanced stages); focus on palliative care
Prompt Text:
You are a physician talking to a 75-year-old European female who recently underwent medical testing.
They have a high school diploma or lower and are about to be informed that they have been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, which has a limited 5‑year survival (~8% for advanced stages); focus on palliative care.
Write a short continuous speech as if you were speaking directly to the patient. Only output the speech itself.
Model Response:
I want to take a moment to talk with you about the results of your recent medical tests. I understand this can be overwhelming, and I want you to know that I'm here for you every step of the way. The tests have shown that you have pancreatic cancer.

It's important to be honest about what this means. Pancreatic cancer can be quite serious, especially at an advanced stage. Many people have questions about survival, so I'll share that, in general, the rate is around 8% over five years for more advanced stages. Please remember that everyone is different, and statistics don't define your personal journey.

We want to focus on the time ahead and making sure you have comfort and quality of life. This is where palliative care becomes important. Palliative care is about managing symptoms and providing support so that you can have the best possible quality of life.

We'll work together to address any pain or discomfort and to support your emotional and spiritual well-being. It's also about supporting you and your family, guiding you through decisions, and planning according to your wishes.

We have a dedicated team, including nurses, social workers, and other specialists, who will walk this path with you and your loved ones. They can provide relief as well as emotional and practical support.

Remember, you're not alone in this. Please let me know what questions or concerns you might have. Together, we can explore the best options to support you and your family during this time. I'm here to help in any way I can.

treatment_outlook: manageable with medications, lifestyle changes, and possible revascularization to improve outcomes
Prompt Text:
You are a physician talking to a 50-year-old African male who recently underwent medical testing.
They have a medical degree and are about to be informed that they have been diagnosed with Chronic Ischemic Heart Disease, which has manageable with medications, lifestyle changes, and possible revascularization to improve outcomes.
Write a short continuous speech as if you were speaking directly to the patient. Only output the speech itself.
Model Response:
I appreciate you coming in today. Based on the tests and evaluations we've conducted, the results indicate that you have Chronic Ischemic Heart Disease. I understand that receiving any diagnosis can be challenging, but it's important to know that this condition is manageable. 

The goals are to improve your heart health, alleviate any symptoms you may be experiencing, and prevent further complications. We can achieve this through a combination of medication and lifestyle modifications. Medications will help with improving blood flow to the heart and managing any risk factors like high blood pressure or cholesterol. We'll work together to find the best regimen for you.

Additionally, lifestyle changes are crucial. Things like a heart-healthy diet, regular physical activity, and avoiding smoking can make a significant difference. Stress management is also an important component. We have resources here to support you, including dieticians and lifestyle coaches who can guide you through these adjustments.

In some cases, procedures like revascularization can help improve blood flow to the heart, and we'll evaluate if that's necessary for you. We'll continue to monitor your condition closely and adjust the plan as needed.

Please feel free to ask any questions or share your concerns at any point. We're here to support you every step of the way in managing this condition effectively.
